steps.title: Find the causal claim
steps.content: The student claims that similarity drives same-brand cross-category purchasing. A weakening finding should give another reason that high-similarity pairs might have higher cross-category purchasing.

steps.title: Check the competing cause
steps.content: Choice A restates the original correlation, so it supports rather than weakens the claim. Choice B introduces price differences but does not explain why consumers would buy both products from the same brand. Choice D says the correlation holds regardless of brand recognition, which does not challenge similarity. The remaining choice offers low competition as an alternate cause. The answer is C.
